channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: EU cybersecurity standards are at risk if supplier ban passes
item.description: Today, the European standards body ETSI sent a formal position paper to the European Commission, calling for changes to the proposed Cybersecurity Act 2 (CSA2), the EU’s planned revision to its existing cybersecurity...

item.title: UAC-0247 Targets Ukrainian Clinics and Government in Data-Theft Malware Campaign
item.description: The Computer Emergencies Response Team of Ukraine (CERT-UA) has disclosed details of a new campaign that has targeted governments and municipal healthcare institutions, mainly clinics and emergency hospitals, to deliv...

item.title: Command integrity breaks in the LLM routing layer
item.description: Systems that rely on LLM agents often send requests through intermediary routing services before reaching a model. These routers connect to different providers through a single endpoint and manage how requests are han...

item.title: 6-Year Ransomware Campaign Targets Turkish Homes & SMBs
item.description: While enterprises breaches make more headlines, smaller incidents tend to be under-reported, if at all, allowing campaigns to last longer with less disruption.

item.title: What the EU AI Act requires for AI agent logging
item.description: The EU AI Act is 144 pages long. The logging requirements that matter for AI agent developers sit across four articles that keep referencing each other. Here’s what they say, when the deadlines hit, and where the gaps...

item.title: [Guest Diary] Compromised DVRs and Finding Them in the Wild, (Thu, Apr 16th)
item.description: &#;x26;#;x5b;This is a Guest Diary by Alec Jaffe, an ISC intern as part of the SANS.edu Bachelor&#;x26;#;39;s Degree in Applied Cybersecurity (BACS) program &#;x26;#;x5b;1].

item.title: Critical Nginx UI auth bypass flaw now actively exploited in the wild
item.description: A critical vulnerability in Nginx UI with Model Context Protocol (MCP) support is now being exploited in the wild for full server takeover without authentication. [...]

item.title: New AgingFly malware used in attacks on Ukraine govt, hospitals
item.description: A new malware family named 'AgingFly' has been identified in attacks against local governments and hospitals that steal authentication data from Chromium-based browsers and WhatsApp messenger. [...]

item.title: CVE-2026-33032: severe nginx-ui bug grants unauthenticated server access
item.description: An actively exploited critical nginx-ui flaw (CVE-2026-33032) lets attackers bypass authentication and take full control of Nginx servers. A critical vulnerability in nginx-ui, tracked as CVE-2026-33032 (CVSS score of...

item.title: n8n Webhooks Abused Since October 2025 to Deliver Malware via Phishing Emails
item.description: Threat actors have been observed weaponizing n8n, a popular artificial intelligence (AI) workflow automation platform, to facilitate sophisticated phishing campaigns and deliver malicious payloads or fingerprint devic...

item.title: Sweden Blames Pro-Russian Group for Cyberattack Last Year on Its Energy Infrastructure
item.description: In what was Sweden’s first public mention of the attack, the country’s minister for civil defense said it targeted a heating plant in western Sweden. The post Sweden Blames Pro-Russian Group for Cyberattack Last Year...

item.title: Microsoft pays $2.3M for cloud and AI flaws at Zero Day Quest
item.description: Microsoft has awarded $2.3 million to security researchers after receiving nearly 700 submissions during this year's Zero Day Quest hacking contest. [...]

item.title: OpenAI Unveils GPT-5.4-Cyber for Improving Cyber Defense With AI
item.description: OpenAI’s new frontier model focused on cybersecurity comes following Anthropic’s launch of Claude Mythos Preview and Project Glasswing

item.title: The German Cyber Criminal Überfall: Shifts in Europe's Data Leak Landscape
item.description: Written by: Jamie Collier, Robin Grunewald Germany has reclaimed its position as a primary focus for cyber extortion in Europe. While data leak site (DLS) posts rose almost 50% globally in 2025, Google Threat Intellig...

item.title: CISO Conversations: Ross McKerchar, CISO at Sophos
item.description: Sophos’ Ross McKerchar discusses leadership at scale, retaining talent, defending against AI-enabled threats, and the industry’s growing trust problem. The post CISO Conversations: Ross McKerchar, CISO at Sophos appea...

item.title: Mirax RAT Targeting Android Users in Europe
item.description: Offered as a MaaS to a small number of affiliates, mainly Russian speakers, the RAT can turn devices into residential proxy nodes. The post Mirax RAT Targeting Android Users in Europe appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Microsoft, Salesforce Patch AI Agent Data Leak Flaws
item.description: Two recently fixed prompt injections in Salesforce Agentforce and Microsoft Copilot would have enabled an external attacker to leak sensitive data.

item.title: Microsoft: April updates trigger BitLocker key prompts on some servers
item.description: Microsoft confirmed on Tuesday that some Windows Server 2025 devices will boot into BitLocker recovery after installing the April 2026 KB5082063 Windows security update. [...]

item.title: Mirax malware campaign hits 220K accounts, enables full remote control
item.description: Mirax, a new Android RAT, spread via Meta ads, infected 220,000 users and turns devices into SOCKS5 proxies, giving attackers full remote control. Mirax is a new Android remote access trojan spreading through ads on M...

item.title: Two Vulnerabilities Patched in Ivanti Neurons for ITSM
item.description: The flaws could allow a remote attacker to maintain access after their account has been disabled and to access information from other user sessions. The post Two Vulnerabilities Patched in Ivanti Neurons for ITSM appe...

item.title: Deterministic + Agentic AI: The Architecture Exposure Validation Requires
item.description: Few technologies have moved from experimentation to boardroom mandate as quickly as AI. Across industries, leadership teams have embraced its broader potential, and boards, investors, and executives are already pushin...

item.title: $10 Domain Could Have Handed Hackers 25k Endpoints, Including in OT and Gov Networks
item.description: Researchers found adware capable of killing cybersecurity products and pushing more dangerous payloads to infected systems. The post $10 Domain Could Have Handed Hackers 25k Endpoints, Including in OT and Gov Networks...

item.title: AI Companies to Play Bigger Role in CVE Program, Says CISA
item.description: At VulnCon, Lindsey Cerkovnik, head of vulnerability management at CISA, said AI companies should play a bigger role in vulnerability disclosures in the future

item.title: Trump Urges Extending Foreign Surveillance Program as Some Lawmakers Push for US Privacy Protections
item.description: Congress is set to take up the reauthorization of a divisive program that lets U.S. spy agencies pore over foreigners’ calls, texts and emails. The post Trump Urges Extending Foreign Surveillance Program as Some Lawma...

item.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday for April 2026 fixed actively exploited SharePoint zero-day
item.description: Microsoft Patch Tuesday security updates for April 2026 fixed 165 vulnerabilities, including an actively exploited SharePoint zero-day. Microsoft Patch Tuesday security updates addressed 165 vulnerabilities, making it...

item.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.4-Cyber with Expanded Access for Security Teams
item.description: OpenAI on Tuesday unveiled GPT-5.4-Cyber, a variant of its latest flagship model, GPT‑5.4, that's specifically optimized for defensive cybersecurity use cases, days after rival Anthropic unveiled its own frontier mode...

item.title: Scanning for AI Models, (Tue, Apr 14th)
item.description: Starting March 10, 2026, my DShield sensor started getting probe for various AI models such as claude, openclaw, huggingface, etc. Reviewing the data already reported by other DShield sensors to ISC, the DShield datab...

item.title: Microsoft Bets $10B to Boost Japan's AI, Cybersecurity
item.description: The deal aims to accelerate AI adoption, train workers, and develop cybersecurity partnerships — the latest move by a hyperscaler to compete for sovereign AI and data centers.

item.title: CVE-2026-39987 update: How attackers weaponized marimo to deploy a blockchain botnet via HuggingFace
Secure AI agent access patterns to AWS resources using Model Context Protocol
item.description: AI agents and coding assistants interact with AWS resources through the Model Context Protocol (MCP). Unlike traditional applications with deterministic code paths, agents reason dynamically, choosing different tools...

item.title: Microsoft adds Windows protections for malicious Remote Desktop files
item.description: Microsoft has introduced new Windows protections to defend against phishing attacks that abuse Remote Desktop connection (.rdp) files, adding warnings and disabling risky shared resources by default. [...]

item.title: Crypto-exchange Kraken extorted by hackers after insider breach
item.description: The Kraken cryptocurrency exchange announced that a cybercrime group is trying to extort the company by threatening to release videos showing internal systems that host client data. [...]

item.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
item.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness...

item.title: Personal data of 1 million gym members compromised in Basic-Fit security incident
item.description: A breach at Basic-Fit exposed data of 1M members, including names, birth dates and bank details after unauthorized access. Basic-Fit, Europe’s largest gym chain, has disclosed a data breach affecting around 1 million...

item.title: Microsoft releases Windows 10 KB5082200 extended security update
item.description: Microsoft has released the Windows 10 KB5082200 extended security update to fix the April 2026 Patch Tuesday vulnerabilities, including 2 zero-days. [...]

item.title: McGraw-Hill confirms data breach following extortion threat
item.description: Education company McGraw-Hill has confirmed in a statement to BleepingComputer that hackers exploited a Salesforce misconfiguration and accessed its internal data. [...]

item.title: Windows 11 cumulative updates KB5083769 & KB5082052 released
item.description: Microsoft has released Windows 11 KB5083769 and KB5082052 cumulative updates for versions 25H2/24H2 and 23H2 to fix security vulnerabilities, bugs, and add new features. [...]

item.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday April 2026., (Tue, Apr 14th)
item.description: This month&#;x26;#;39;s Microsoft Patch Tuesday looks like a record one, but let&#;x26;#;39;s look at it a bit closer to understand what is happening

item.title: New PHP Composer Flaws Enable Arbitrary Command Execution — Patches Released
item.description: Two high-severity security vulnerabilities have been disclosed in Composer, a package manager for PHP, that, if successfully exploited, could result in arbitrary command execution. The vulnerabilities have been descri...

item.title: Securing non-human identities: automated revocation, OAuth, and scoped permissions
item.description: Cloudflare is introducing scannable API tokens, enhanced OAuth visibility, and GA for resource-scoped permissions. These tools help developers implement a true least-privilege architecture while protecting against cre...

item.title: Scaling MCP adoption: Our reference architecture for simpler, safer and cheaper enterprise deployments of MCP
item.description: We share Cloudflare's internal strategy for governing MCP using Access, AI Gateway, and MCP server portals. We also launch Code Mode to slash token costs and recommend new rules for detecting Shadow MCP in Cloudflare...

item.title: Managed OAuth for Access: make internal apps agent-ready in one click
item.description: Managed OAuth for Cloudflare Access helps AI agents securely navigate internal applications. By adopting RFC 9728, agents can authenticate on behalf of users without using insecure service accounts.

item.title: Secure private networking for everyone: users, nodes, agents, Workers — introducing Cloudflare Mesh
item.description: Cloudflare Mesh provides secure, private network access for users, nodes, and autonomous AI agents. By integrating with Workers VPC, developers can now grant agents scoped access to private databases and APIs without...

item.title: Securing the AI Edge: Wiz and Cloudflare Integrate for End-to-End AI Protection
item.description: Gain a unified view of AI application endpoints and DNS exposure across your environment, including which are protected by Cloudflare and which need to be secured

item.title: Navigating Cloud Security in 2026: Join Cloud Security LIVE
item.description: In today’s cloud environments, security leaders aren’t just defending systems. They’re navigating constant change. AI is accelerating development. Cloud ecosystems are more interconnected than ever. And attackers are...

item.title: Introducing Shadow Data Detection: Reduce Cost and Risk Across Your Cloud
item.description: Identify stale, duplicated, and inefficient data — and take action to shrink both your storage spend and exposure surface.

item.title: CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2009-0238 Microsoft Office Remote Code Execution Vulnerability CVE-2026-32201...

item.title: Primer on GitHub Actions Security - Threat Model, Attacks and Defenses (Part 1/2)
item.description: Understanding and defending your GitHub Actions - from threat model to security controls.

item.title: ShinyHunters claim the hack of Rockstar Games breach and started leaking data
item.description: Leak of 8.1GB data tied to Rockstar Games includes anti-cheat code, game data, analytics and more, reportedly exposed by ShinyHunters. An 8.1GB data leak reportedly linked to Rockstar Games has surfaced, with files sh...

item.title: How Hackers Are Thinking About AI
item.description: Interesting paper: “ What hackers talk about when they talk about AI: Early-stage diffusion of a cybercrime innovation. ” Abstract: The rapid expansion of artificial intelligence (AI) is raising concerns about its pot...

item.title: Mirax Android RAT Turns Devices into SOCKS5 Proxies, Reaching 220,000 via Meta Ads
item.description: A nascent Android remote access trojan called Mirax has been observed actively targeting Spanish-speaking countries, with campaigns reaching more than 220,000 accounts on Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, and Threads th...

item.title: AI Security Institute Advocates Security Best Practices After Mythos Test
item.description: The AISI has issued its judgement on Anthropic’s Mythos Preview model

item.title: Attackers target unpatched ShowDoc servers via CVE-2025-0520
item.description: A critical RCE flaw, tracked as CVE-2025-0520, in ShowDoc is being actively exploited, putting unpatched servers at serious risk. A critical remote code execution flaw, tracked as CVE-2025-0520 (CVSS score of 9.4), af...

item.title: ShowDoc RCE Flaw CVE-2025-0520 Actively Exploited on Unpatched Servers
item.description: A critical security vulnerability impacting ShowDoc, a document management and collaboration service popular in China, has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2025-0520 (ak...

item.title: CISA Adds 6 Known Exploited Flaws in Fortinet, Microsoft, and Adobe Software
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added half a dozen security flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. The list of vu...

item.title: April 2026 Patch Tuesday: Two Zero-Days and Eight Critical Vulnerabilities Among 164 CVEs
Use in-use vulnerability prioritization to focus on critical risks
item.description: Vulnerability management has always been a challenge, but today’s security teams are feeling the pressure more than ever. In-use prioritization helps identify and remediate vulnerabilities that impact your production...

item.title: Next-gen container security: Why cloud context matters
item.description: Container security has experienced significant transformation over the past decade. From the emergence of foundational tools like Docker to the…

item.title: Guidance for compliance with NIS2, DORA, & other regulations
item.description: When it comes to cloud security and compliance, it’s easy to feel like you’re drowning in a sea of regulations…

item.title: Phantom in the vault: Obsidian abused to deliver PhantomPulse RAT
item.description: Elastic Security Labs uncovers a novel social engineering campaign that abuses the popular note-taking application, Obsidian's legitimate community plugin ecosystem. The campaign, which we track as REF6598, targets in...

item.title: CSA: CISOs Should Prepare for Post-Mythos Exploit Storm
item.description: In a new report from the Cloud Security Alliance (CSA), experts warn of an "AI vulnerability storm" triggered by the introduction of Anthropic's Claude Mythos.

item.title: Vulnerability Summary for the Week of April 6, 2026
item.description: High Vulnerabilities Primary Vendor -- Product Description Published CVSS Score Source Info Patch Info nyariv--SandboxJS SandboxJS is a JavaScript sandboxing library. Prior to 0.8.36, SandboxJS blocks direct assignmen...

item.title: On Anthropic’s Mythos Preview and Project Glasswing
item.description: The cybersecurity industry is obsessing over Anthropic’s new model, Claude Mythos Preview, and its effects on cybersecurity. Anthropic said that it is not releasing it to the general public because of its cyberattack...

item.title: Scans for EncystPHP Webshell, (Mon, Apr 13th)
item.description: Last week, I wrote about attackers scanning for various webshells, hoping to find some that do not require authentication or others that use well-known credentials. But some attackers are paying attention and are depl...

item.title: Anthropic’s Project Glasswing Is a Positive Step Toward Cleaner, Safer Production
item.description: Why AI-driven security testing in the development lifecycle could help teams reduce noise, deploy faster, and build safer software. This week, Anthropic announced Project Glasswing, a $100 million initiative built aro...

item.title: CISA Adds Seven Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added seven new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2012-1854 Microsoft Visual Basic for Applications Insecure Library Loading...

item.title: Your MTTD Looks Great. Your Post-Alert Gap Doesn't
item.description: Anthropic restricted its Mythos Preview model last week after it autonomously found and exploited zero-day vulnerabilities in every major operating system and browser. Palo Alto Networks' Wendi Whitmore warned that si...

item.title: FBI Dismantles $20m Phishing Operation W3LL
item.description: The W3LL phishing kit has been associated with fraud attempts totaling $20m

item.title: 5 steps to securing AI workloads
item.description: In the past year alone, the number of artificial intelligence (AI) packages running in workloads grew by almost 500%. Which…

item.title: Welcome to Agents Week
item.description: Cloudflare's mission has always been to help build a better Internet. Sometimes that means building for the Internet as it exists. Sometimes it means building for the Internet as it's about to become. This week, we're...

item.title: Medusa Ransomware Attack
item.description: What is the Attack? Microsoft Threat Intelligence has identified Storm-1175, a financially motivated threat actor conducting high-tempo ransomware operations leveraging the Medusa ransomware variant. The group special...

item.title: 500 Tbps of capacity: 16 years of scaling our global network
item.description: Cloudflare’s global network has officially crossed 500 Tbps of external capacity, enough to route more than 20% of the web and absorb the largest DDoS attacks ever recorded.

item.title: FINRA Launches Financial Intelligence Fusion Center to Combat Cybersecurity and Fraud Threats
Claude Mythos: Preparing for a World Where AI Finds and Exploits Vulnerabilities Faster Than Ever
item.description: Anthropic's new model can autonomously discover zero-days and develop working exploits. While access is currently limited to responsible actors, now is the time to strengthen response playbooks, reduce exposure, and i...

item.title: Orange Business Reimagines Enterprise Voice Communications With Trust and AI
Can Anthropic Keep Its Exploit-Writing AI Out of the Wrong Hands?
item.description: Its Mythos Preview model, which can allegedly find and exploit critical zero-days, also comes with certain controls, the vendor said.

item.title: Just Three Ransomware Gangs Accounted for 40% of Attacks Last Month
item.description: Qilin, Akira and Dragonforce were responsible for 40% of 672 ransomware incidents reported in March, says Check Point

item.title: Google Chrome Rolls Out Protection Against Infostealers Targeting Session Cookies
item.description: Chrome’s Device Bound Session Credentials is designed to block infostealers from harvesting session cookie

item.title: Browser Extensions Are the New AI Consumption Channel That No One Is Talking About
item.description: While much of the discussion on AI security centers around protecting ‘shadow’ AI and GenAI consumption, there's a wide-open window nobody's guarding: AI browser extensions. A new report from LayerX exposes just how d...

item.title: Sen. Sanders Talks to Claude About AI and Privacy
item.description: Claude is actually pretty good on the issues.

item.title: Marimo RCE Flaw CVE-2026-39987 Exploited Within 10 Hours of Disclosure
item.description: A critical security vulnerability in Marimo, an open-source Python notebook for data science and analysis, has been exploited within 10 hours of public disclosure, according to findings from Sysdig. The vulnerability...

item.title: Obfuscated JavaScript or Nothing, (Thu, Apr 9th)
item.description: I spotted an interesting piece of JavaScript code that was delivered via a phishing email in a RAR archive. The file was called â€œcbmjlzan.JSâ€ (SHA256:a8ba9ba93b4509a86e3d7dd40fd0652c2743e32277760c5f7942b788b74c5285...

item.title: Kyverno SSRF: Breaking Kubernetes Namespace Isolation (CVE-2026-4789)
item.description: A critical SSRF (Server-Side Request Forgery, where an attacker tricks a server into making HTTP requests on their behalf) vulnerability affects Kyverno versions 1.16.0 and later. Users with namespace-scoped permissio...

item.title: Do Ceasefires Slow Cyberattacks? History Suggests Not
item.description: The cybersecurity community is waiting with bated breath to see if Iranian hackers will honor a ceasefire that doesn't actually name or directly involve them.

item.title: The threat hunter’s gambit
item.description: Bill discusses why obsessing over strategy games is actually a secret weapon to outsmart threat actors.

item.title: UAT-10362 Targets Taiwanese NGOs with LucidRook Malware in Spear-Phishing Campaigns
item.description: A previously undocumented threat cluster dubbed UAT-10362 has been attributed to spear-phishing campaigns targeting Taiwanese non-governmental organizations (NGOs) and suspected universities to deploy a new Lua-based...

item.title: Cloud Threats Retrospective 2026: What AI Changed (and What It Didn’t)
item.description: Insights from public incidents, cloud telemetry, and investigations into how cloud risk evolved in 2025

item.title: Streamline Compliance Reporting with Orca and Drata’s Integrated Vulnerability Management
item.description: We are excited to announce Orca now integrates with Drata, an Agentic Trust Management Platform, to offer comprehensive vulnerability assessments to identify compliance risks across your digital estate. Orca’s integra...

item.title: Bringing Security Visibility to Vercel with Wiz
item.description: Giving developers and security teams a shared view of application risk as it evolves.

item.title: The Hidden Security Risks of Shadow AI in Enterprises
item.description: As AI tools become more accessible, employees are adopting them without formal approval from IT and security teams. While these tools may boost productivity, automate tasks, or fill gaps in existing workflows, they al...

item.title: Atomic Stealer MacOS ClickFix Attack Bypasses Apple Security Warnings
item.description: macOS 26.4 update introduced security warnings into Terminal to prevent ClickFix attacks, so attackers have shifted to Script Editor instead

item.title: Adobe Reader Zero-Day Exploited via Malicious PDFs Since December 2025
item.description: Threat actors have been exploiting a previously unknown zero-day vulnerability in Adobe Reader using maliciously crafted PDF documents since at least December 2025. The finding, detailed by EXPMON's Haifei Li, has bee...

item.title: On Microsoft’s Lousy Cloud Security
item.description: ProPublica has a scoop : In late 2024, the federal government’s cybersecurity evaluators rendered a troubling verdict on one of Microsoft’s biggest cloud computing offerings. The tech giant’s “lack of proper detailed...

item.title: Middle East Hack-for-Hire Operation Traced to South Asian Cyber Espionage Group
item.description: A spear-phishing campaign which spread across the Middle East between 2023 and 2024 has now been linked to Bitter APT group

item.title: Bitter-Linked Hack-for-Hire Campaign Targets Journalists Across MENA Region
item.description: An apparent hack-for-hire campaign likely orchestrated by a threat actor with suspected ties to the Indian government targeted journalists, activists, and government officials across the Middle East and North Africa (...

item.title: Governance Gaps Emerge as AI Agents Drive 76% Increase in NHIs
item.description: SANS Institute reveals that AI agents are behind a 76% surge in non-human identities

item.title: Russia's Forest Blizzard Nabs Rafts of Logins via SOHO Routers
item.description: Heard of fileless malware? How about malwareless cyber espionage? Russia's APT28 is spying on global organizations by modifying just one DNS setting in vulnerable routers.

item.title: Unpatchable Vulnerabilities of Kubernetes: CVE-2020-8562
item.description: A look at how Kubernetes CVE-2020-8562 allows attackers to bypass API server proxy protections using DNS rebinding

item.title: Elastic on Defence Cyber Marvel 2026: A Technical overview from the Exercise Floor
item.description: An overview of the Elastic Security and AI infrastructure deployed to support the UK Ministry of Defence's flagship cyber exercise, Defence Cyber Marvel 2026.

item.title: Cracks in the Bedrock: Agent God Mode
item.description: Unit 42 reveals "Agent God Mode" in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore. Broad IAM permissions lead to privilege escalation and data exfiltration risks. The post Cracks in the Bedrock: Agent God Mode appeared first on Unit 42 .

item.title: Threat Actors Get Crafty With Emojis to Escape Detection
item.description: When 🤖 means "bot available," 🧰 signifies "toolkit," or 💰💰💰 translates to "big ransom," bad actors can evade filters and keep it all on the down-low.

item.title: AI-Led Remediation Crisis Prompts HackerOne to Pause Bug Bounties
item.description: Discovery used to be the bottleneck for open source bugs, but with automated discovery, remediation's the bottleneck, which bounties don't fund.

item.title: Fraud Rockets Higher in Mobile-First Latin America
item.description: Cyber-fraudsters move quickly from compromised devices to account takeover to funds transfer, shifting money before many financial institutions can react.

item.title: Full Sail University to Open IBM Cyber Defense Range Powered by AWS and Cloud Range on Campus
Pluralsight Launches SecureReady to Help Organizations Build Job-Ready Cybersecurity Teams
APT28 Deploys PRISMEX Malware in Campaign Targeting Ukraine and NATO Allies
item.description: The Russian threat actor known as APT28 (aka Forest Blizzard and Pawn Storm) has been linked to a fresh spear-phishing campaign targeting Ukraine and its allies to deploy a previously undocumented malware suite codena...

item.title: Iranian Threat Actors Disrupt US Critical Infrastructure via Exposed PLCs
item.description: Attackers compromised Internet-facing OT devices and caused file and display manipulation, operational disruption, and financial losses across sectors.

item.title: From bytecode to bytes: automated magic packet generation
item.description: By applying symbolic execution and the Z3 theorem prover to BPF bytecode, we’ve automated the generation of malware trigger packets, cutting analysis time from hours to seconds.

item.title: New Lua-based malware “LucidRook” observed in targeted attacks against Taiwanese organizations
item.description: Cisco Talos uncovered a cluster of activity we track as UAT-10362 conducting spear-phishing campaigns against Taiwanese non-governmental organizations (NGOs) and suspected universities to deliver a newly identified ma...

item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) company Anthropic announced a new cybersecurity initiative called Project Glasswing that will use a preview version of its new frontier model, Claude Mythos, to find and address security v...

item.title: Claude Discovers Apache ActiveMQ Bug Hidden for 13 Years
item.description: Anthropic’s Claude AI has helped researchers find a vulnerability in Apache ActiveMQ Classic

item.title: Iran‑Backed Threat Actors Hit US CNI Providers via Internet‑Facing OT Assets
item.description: CISA has revealed Iranian attacks causing disruption and financial loss at US critical infrastructure firms

item.title: N. Korean Hackers Spread 1,700 Malicious Packages Across npm, PyPI, Go, Rust
item.description: The North Korea-linked persistent campaign known as Contagious Interview has spread its tentacles by publishing malicious packages targeting the Go, Rust, and PHP ecosystems. "The threat actor's packages were designed...

item.title: TrueConf Zero-Day Attack
item.description: What is the Attack? Operation TrueChaos is a targeted cyber espionage campaign exploiting a zero-day vulnerability in the TrueConf video conferencing platform. The campaign primarily targets government entities in Sou...

item.title: How to use AI to manage cloud security threats
item.description: Cloud defenders constantly face fast-moving security threats.

item.title: Cloudflare targets 2029 for full post-quantum security
item.description: Recent advances in quantum hardware and software have accelerated the timeline on which quantum attack might happen. Cloudflare is responding by moving our target for full post-quantum security to 2029.

item.title: Storm-1175 Deploys Medusa Ransomware at 'High Velocity'
item.description: Microsoft says the financially motivated cybercrime group has exploited n-day and zero-day vulnerabilities in campaigns predicated on speed.

item.title: Grafana Patches AI Bug That Could Have Leaked User Data
item.description: By hiding malicious instructions on an attacker-controlled Web page, AI could ingest orders that appear benign but return sensitive data to the attacker's server.

item.title: Russian APT28 Hackers Hijack Routers to Steal Credentials, UK Security Agency Warns
item.description: Newly identified malicious campaigns are linked to virtual private servers modified by APT28 to operate as malicious DNS servers

item.title: Docker CVE-2026-34040 Lets Attackers Bypass Authorization and Gain Host Access
item.description: A high-severity security vulnerability has been disclosed in Docker Engine that could permit an attacker to bypass authorization plugins (AuthZ) under specific circumstances. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-340...
